The breakthrough idea is well-specified and physically grounded (Knowles secondary-nucleation formalism). Let me verify the mechanistic structure against the actual amyloid master equation, then implement.

The Knowles/Cohen framework: dM/dt has contributions from primary nucleation (∝ m^n_c), elongation (∝ P·m, fibril-end growth), and secondary nucleation (∝ M·m^n_2, surface-catalyzed). Inhibitors act on specific microscopic steps. The idea to apply inhibition only to the autocatalytic channels (elongation + secondary) while leaving primary source intact is the key mechanistic insight — it lets cd=0 recover clean kinetics and high cd delay the sigmoidal takeoff.

Let me do a quick targeted research check to confirm the microscopic-step inhibition principle before finalizing.
